stories.title: Social Security payments will look a bit different in August 2026
stories.summary: Social Security recipients will receive two checks in July instead of one due to the August 1 payment date falling on a Saturday. As a result, no checks will be issued in August, with the next payment scheduled for September 1. This adjustment affects Supplemental Security Income recipients, while those receiving Social Security benefits based on their date of birth will follow a separate payment schedule.

stories.title: Appeals court blocks Trump administration from revoking TPS for Haitians until at least Monday
stories.summary: A federal appeals court has temporarily blocked the Trump administration from revoking deportation protections for over 300,000 Haitian migrants living in the US, at least until Monday, July 27. The court's decision comes after a lower court order postponed the TPS revocations while legal challenges continued. The administration's request to strip TPS was denied, allowing Haitian migrants to remain in the US for now.

stories.title: WATCH: Trump expands pledge aimed at shielding consumers from energy cost hikes due to data centers
stories.summary: President Donald Trump has expanded his pledge to shield US consumers from higher utility bills caused by data centers, with 23 governors signing on to the voluntary commitment. The pledge aims to protect households from absorbing costs tied to data centers, which are powering artificial intelligence and expanding rapidly across the country. Trump's Ratepayer Protection Pledge now includes nearly 200 stakeholders, with the goal of lowering electricity costs for consumers in the long term.

stories.title: Indiana Fever asked to 'keep quiet' about 2025 harassment firing, former player says
stories.summary: A former Indiana Fever player has alleged that the team fired a male employee for sexual harassment in 2025 and instructed players to keep quiet about the incident. The player, Brianna Turner, made the claim on social media, stating that sexism, misogyny, homophobia, and racism are all issues that need to be addressed. The Indiana Fever have since confirmed that protecting their players and employees is their top priority, but have not commented on the specific allegations.

stories.title: 'A Toxic Love Story' is the wildest true crime doc Netflix has released in years
stories.summary: A new Netflix true crime documentary, A Toxic Love Story, has been released, following a series of bizarre and shocking events. The documentary, directed by Alexandra Lacey, centers around Michelle Hadley, who shares her side of the story, and features three major reveals that challenge initial perceptions. The case, which does not result in any fatalities, is considered one of the most unusual in a string of recent Netflix true crime documentaries.

stories.title: Ready to 'conquer Earth' - Joshua embraces comeback spotlight
stories.summary: Anthony Joshua returns to the ring Saturday in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, facing Albanian heavyweight Kristian Prenga in his first fight since a December car crash in Nigeria killed two team members. A win would set up an all-British heavyweight showdown with Tyson Fury, pending Fury’s own fight Friday in Thailand. Joshua called his comeback a step toward “conquering Earth” before a packed crowd in Jeddah.

stories.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
stories.summary: France evacuated 20,000 people in the Gironde region on Tuesday as wildfires scorched 3,400 hectares near the Bay of Arcachon. Authorities moved 8,800 residents and tourists from campsites and villages, with no injuries reported. Firefighters warned conditions would worsen by evening as temperatures rose and winds strengthened.

stories.title: Burnham says 20% business rates cut for English pubs 'a first step'
stories.summary: New Prime Minister Andy Burnham has announced a 20% cut to business rates for English pubs, social clubs, and live music venues, set to take effect from April, with an estimated annual savings of £1,100 per firm, and a total cost of £100m. The move is expected to benefit nearly 32,000 venues, but will not apply to the very largest live music venues or nightclubs. The government will review tax relief on businesses like vape shops to fund the cut.

stories.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
stories.summary: NASA has expanded its streaming platform, NASA+, to Amazon Fire TV Channels, allowing customers to access its content through Fire TV devices and compatible Alexa-enabled devices. The platform, which remains available for free on the NASA app and website, will feature a range of programming including science mission launches and the first crewed mission to the Moon under the Artemis program. This move aligns with NASA's mission to share its story of space exploration with the broadest possible audience.

stories.title: Help for electricity bills as crude oil hits $100
stories.summary: Alderney will apply £1.2 million in public funds directly to household and business electricity bills to offset rising costs after crude oil hit $100 a barrel. The temporary subsidy targets diesel-generated power, the island’s sole source since it lacks a French cable link. The committee stressed this measure supports, not replaces, ongoing efforts to secure cheaper fuel contracts.

stories.title: LISTEN: How Travis Scott is Ascending to Culture Moguldom Through Music, Movies, Retail, Business and More
stories.summary: Travis Scott is emerging as a culture mogul, following a record-setting music tour and a high-profile role in Christopher Nolan's film "The Odyssey." He has also partnered with major brands like McDonald's and has a range of business ventures in his hometown of Houston. Scott is now set to produce his own movie and TV shows through a first-look deal with Paramount Pictures.

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.summary: Anthropic has expanded voice mode to its Opus and Sonnet AI models, enabling deeper problem-solving and task automation. Users can now switch between voice and text mid-conversation and toggle models as needed. The update follows last year’s launch of voice mode for Haiku, which focused on quick responses.

stories.title: Patreon Lays Off 20% of Employees as Part of ‘Painful’ Restructuring
stories.summary: Patreon is cutting 20% of its workforce, laying off 93 employees with at least 16 weeks of severance. The restructuring aims to stabilize the platform amid market shifts affecting the creator economy. The company says its core business remains strong as it adjusts its cost structure.

stories.title: Italy confirm Ancelotti approach before Guardiola
stories.summary: Italy first approached Carlo Ancelotti to become their national team manager before considering Pep Guardiola. The Azzurri have been without a head coach since April after failing to qualify for a third straight World Cup. Ancelotti, currently Brazil’s manager, remains in charge until the 2030 World Cup.

stories.title: L.A. Production Volume Down Slightly, as Recovery Continues to Sputter
stories.summary: Los Angeles production volume slipped 3.1% in the second quarter of 2026 despite a state tax incentive boost. Film and commercial shoots led the decline while TV production edged up, though all remain below historic levels. Reality TV and dramas showed gains, signaling uneven recovery across the industry.

stories.title: Modric, 40, signs new AC Milan deal
stories.summary: Luka Modric, 40, has signed a one-year contract extension with AC Milan. The Croatian midfielder, who turns 41 in September, joins Milan last summer and scored two goals in 37 appearances last season. He expressed excitement to continue playing for the club and help them bounce back in the new campaign.

stories.title: Trump Throws a Potential Wrench in the Saudi Nuclear Deal He Just Inked
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has long said that a condition of normalizing relations with Israel would be a pathway toward Palestinian statehood, a prospect that Israel’s current government has rejected.

stories.title: The Tate Brothers Are Losing Right-Wing Allies After Their Arrest
stories.summary: After Andrew and Tristan Tate, prominent leaders of the so-called manosphere, were arrested in Miami, many of their onetime allies on the American right pulled back support.

stories.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
stories.summary: Patreon is cutting 20 percent of its workforce, or about 93 jobs, the company confirmed today. CEO Jack Conte cited AI-driven industry changes as the reason for the layoffs, not job replacement. The company plans to continue using AI tools to support creators despite the staff reductions.

stories.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
stories.summary: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: U.S. to Withdraw Subpoenas Issued to New York Times Journalists
stories.summary: A federal judge raised questions about the government’s handling of the subpoenas, which The Times said were an attempt to intimidate the free press.

stories.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Saudi Arabia must recognize Israel to secure a U.S. nuclear energy deal. The agreement, which bars uranium enrichment, hinges on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords. Saudi officials have not yet responded to the condition.

stories.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
stories.summary: Shares of Google and Tesla plummeted on Thursday, with Alphabet's parent company experiencing a 7% drop and Tesla's stock falling 13.5% after both companies reported negative free cash flow in financial results. Alphabet now expects to spend up to $205 billion this year on AI projects and infrastructure, a $15 billion increase from its previous estimate. This massive spending spree has left investors wondering when financial benefits will materialize from these investments.

stories.title: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
stories.summary: The final trailer for Coyote vs. Acme drops at San Diego Comic-Con today, with the animated film set for theaters on August 28, 2026. The movie follows Wile E. Coyote’s legal battle against Acme Corporation after repeated failures to catch the Road Runner. Warner Bros. plans a wide release following the SDCC premiere.

stories.title: Key Republican Senator Says He Will Support Erica Schwartz for C.D.C. Director
stories.summary: Senator Bill Cassidy, who is believed to hold the deciding vote in the Senate health committee for Dr. Erica Schwartz’s confirmation, had declined for days to say whether he would back her.

stories.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
stories.summary: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.

stories.title: BBC Inside Science
stories.summary: India's landmark Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, or Pocso, has seen reported cases surge into the tens of thousands each year since its introduction in 2012 following the Delhi gang rape. The law, aimed at exposing hidden abuse, trafficking, and child marriage, is now being applied to teenagers in consensual relationships, resulting in investigations, trials, and potential long prison terms. This has sparked controversy over the country's age of consent.

stories.title: The only way to stop petrol pump thieves | Brief letters
stories.summary: A new UK-wide crackdown on petrol pump theft starts today, requiring drivers to pay upfront or leave a card deposit before fueling. Police report a 15% rise in drive-offs this year, costing forecourts £30 million annually. Forecourt owners warn pumps will lock until payment is confirmed.

stories.title: ‘Odyssey’ Popcorn Buckets Help Fuel a $100 Million Market
stories.summary: Once, a tub of popcorn was just a tub of popcorn. Now, ‘collectible concession vessels’ could make one chain more than $100 million.

stories.title: Risk of Parkinson’s disease may increase with prolonged exposure to road traffic noise
stories.summary: A new study has found that prolonged exposure to road traffic noise may increase the risk of Parkinson's disease, with a 3% higher risk for every 11.5 decibel rise in noise level at the most exposed facade. The study, which included 3.1 million Danish participants aged 40 and over, followed them for 18 years and suggests that having a quiet part of the home may mitigate this risk.

stories.title: Poorest children in England 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections
stories.summary: Children in England’s poorest areas are 40% more likely to be hospitalised for lung infections than those in wealthier regions, new NHS data shows. In 2024-25, 1,941 per 100,000 children from the most deprived fifth were admitted compared to 1,380 per 100,000 in the least deprived. Experts warn air pollution and deprivation are key drivers behind the disparity.

offbeat.title: Social Security payments will look a bit different in August 2026

As a result, no checks will be issued in August, with the next payment scheduled for September 1.
